perturbation.original_text: Developer will design, develop, and deliver, satisfactory to Client, the "Pelican Delivers Application Phase 1" (collectively, the Subject Program), and all elated Project services (collectively, the Services), Project work product (collectively, the Deliverables), and user manuals and other written material that describe he functionality or assist in the use of the Subject Program (collectively, the Documentation), pursuant to the Project specifications detailed in the SO (collectively, Specifications), as described in the enclosed statement of work (the SOW; as provided in APPENDIX A hereto).
perturbation.changed_text: Developer will design, develop, and deliver, satisfactory to Client, the "Pelican Delivers Application Phase 1" (collectively, the Designated System), and all elated Project services (collectively, the Services), Project work product (collectively, the Deliverables), and user manuals and other written material that describe he functionality or assist in the use of the Designated System (collectively, the Documentation), pursuant to the Project specifications detailed in the SO (collectively, Specifications), as described in the enclosed statement of work (the SOW; as provided in APPENDIX A hereto).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Subject Program' is changed to 'Designated System'. While they refer to the same thing, the contract consistently uses 'Subject Program'. The change introduces confusion, especially in sections that reference 'Subject Program' and create doubt as to whether this change also carries across all mentions within the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PROJECT DELIVERY AND INSTALLATION As detailed in a SOW hereto, Developer will provide certain Services, Deliverables and Documentation to Client upon a designated Milestone in accordance with such Milestone's specific Specifications (collectively, Specific Specifications). Prior to completing a Milestone, Developer will: (a) inform Client of the availability of each portion of a Deliverable otherwise required be delivered by such Milestone date for testing by Client (he Acceptance Test Date); and (b) deliver to Client sue Deliverable (each a Milestone Deliverable) including the source code and object code form compatible with the platform(s) described in the SOW for such Milestone Deliverable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Subject Program' with 'Designated System', while other parts of the contract, specifically the 'Contradicted Text', still refer to 'Subject Program'. This misalignment of terminology introduces a contradiction because it creates uncertainty regarding which term applies where. A reasonable reader would be unclear whether the 'Milestone Deliverable' should conform to the 'Subject Program' or 'Designated System' standards, as the contract now uses these terms inconsistently, thus meeting the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (a Change Request), within ten (10) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.changed_text: Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (an Alteration Notice), within ten (10) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Change Request' is changed to 'Alteration Notice' in the first sentence. However, the subsequent sentences in the same paragraph still use the term 'Change Request'. This creates confusion as to whether an 'Alteration Notice' is the same as a 'Change Request,' potentially invalidating the acceptance clause if an 'Alteration Notice' is issued but not treated as a 'Change Request'. This introduces ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ACCEPTANCE OF MILESTONE DELIVERABLE; CHANGE REQUESTS Client will notify Developer in writing of any failure of a Milestone Deliverable to comply with the Specifications, or of any other objections, corrections, changes or amendments required (an Alteration Notice), within ten (10) days of such Milestone's Acceptance Test Date. Any Change Request shall be sufficient to identify, with clarity, any objection, correction, change or amendment to such Milestone Deliverable. In the absence of a Change Request from Client within the time periods detailed herein, the Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted by Client.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Change Request' with 'Alteration Notice' in the first sentence of the section describing acceptance of milestone deliverables and the process for requesting changes. However, subsequent sentences in the same paragraph retain the original term 'Change Request', creating a clear contradiction. The paragraph now references two different terms, 'Alteration Notice' and 'Change Request', without clarifying if they are interchangeable. This ambiguity introduces uncertainty regarding the requirements for a valid change request and whether an 'Alteration Notice' fulfills those requirements. This directly impacts the enforceability of the milestone acceptance clause because it is unclear which type of notice is sufficient to prevent deemed acceptance.

perturbation.original_text: The Client and the Designer both agree to never to disparage or speak ill of the other party to anyone and or post negative or disparaging comments Online regarding any of the Designer's products, services, affiliates, subsidiaries, officers, directors, employees or shareholders, and will take reasonable steps to prevent and will not knowingly permit any of their respective employees or agents to, disparage or speak ill of such persons. For purposes of this Section, "disparage" shall mean any negative statement whether written or oral, about Seattle Software Developers, Inc., Dot Com LLC. and or any of its affiliates. The Client and Designer both agree not to post on defamatory websites or review websites any negative posts concerning each other, the names of our companies, and our employees. Both the Client and the Designer both agree and acknowledge that this non-disparagement provision is a material term of this Agreement, the absence of which would have resulted in the Company refusing to enter into this Agreement. Subject to the terms, conditions, express representations and warranties provided in this Agreement, Designer and Client both agree to indemnify, save and hold armless each other from any and all damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arising out of any finding of fact which is inconsistent with Designer's representations and warranties made herein, except in the event any such claims, damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arise directly as a result of gross negligence or misconduct of Client.
perturbation.changed_text: The Client and the Designer both agree to never to slander or speak ill of the other party to anyone and or post negative or disparaging comments Online regarding any of the Designer's products, services, affiliates, subsidiaries, officers, directors, employees or shareholders, and will take reasonable steps to prevent and will not knowingly permit any of their respective employees or agents to, disparage or speak ill of such persons. For purposes of this Section, "disparage" shall mean any negative statement whether written or oral, about Seattle Software Developers, Inc., Dot Com LLC. and or any of its affiliates. The Client and Designer both agree not to post on defamatory websites or review websites any negative posts concerning each other, the names of our companies, and our employees. Both the Client and the Designer both agree and acknowledge that this non-disparagement provision is a material term of this Agreement, the absence of which would have resulted in the Company refusing to enter into this Agreement. Subject to the terms, conditions, express representations and warranties provided in this Agreement, Designer and Client both agree to indemnify, save and hold armless each other from any and all damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arising out of any finding of fact which is inconsistent with Designer's representations and warranties made herein, except in the event any such claims, damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arise directly as a result of gross negligence or misconduct of Client.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'disparage' is changed to 'slander' in the first sentence. While they are similar, 'disparage' is the defined term later in the paragraph. Using 'slander' introduces a term that's legally distinct but not defined within this specific clause, potentially weakening the non-disparagement clause. The rest of the section defines the term "disparage", this is an intext contradiction as the initial sentence mentions "slander" with no definition
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON DISPARAGEMENT CLAUSE The Client and the Designer both agree to never to slander or speak ill of the other party to anyone and or post negative or disparaging comments Online regarding any of the Designer's products, services, affiliates, subsidiaries, officers, directors, employees or shareholders, and will take reasonable steps to prevent and will not knowingly permit any of their respective employees or agents to, disparage or speak ill of such persons. For purposes of this Section, "disparage" shall mean any negative statement whether written or oral, about Seattle Software Developers, Inc., Dot Com LLC. and or any of its affiliates. The Client and Designer both agree not to post on defamatory websites or review websites any negative posts concerning each other, the names of our companies, and our employees. Both the Client and the Designer both agree and acknowledge that this non-disparagement provision is a material term of this Agreement, the absence of which would have resulted in the Company refusing to enter into this Agreement. Subject to the terms, conditions, express representations and warranties provided in this Agreement, Designer and Client both agree to indemnify, save and hold armless each other from any and all damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arising out of any finding of fact which is inconsistent with Designer's representations and warranties made herein, except in the event any such claims, damages, liabilities, costs, losses or expenses arise directly as a result of gross negligence or misconduct of Client.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'disparage', which is then defined. The changed text replaces 'disparage' with 'slander' in the first sentence. 'Slander' is a legally distinct term from 'disparage', but it is not defined within the clause. Because 'slander' is not defined in the section that is supposed to explain disparagement, the changed text contradicts the purpose of the section, as the section intends to only refer to disparagement.
